function_match.technique: Multiplicative boost when function class matches target profile

source_to_target.control_title: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood, managed, and documented.
Trustworthy AI characteristics integrated into organizational policies, processes, and procedures.
Processes and procedures in place to determine needed level of risk management activities based on organizational risk t
Risk management process and outcomes established through transparent policies, procedures, and controls based on organiz
Ongoing monitoring and periodic review of risk management process and outcomes; roles and responsibilities defined.
Mechanisms in place to inventory AI systems, resourced according to organizational risk priorities.
Processes and procedures for decommissioning and phasing out AI systems safely without increasing risks.
Roles, responsibilities, and lines of communication related to mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks documented and
Personnel and partners receive AI risk management training to perform duties consistent with policies and procedures.
Executive leadership takes responsibility for decisions about risks associated with AI system development and deployment
Decision-making informed by diverse team reflecting demographic, disciplinary, and experiential diversity.
Policies and procedures define and differentiate roles for human-AI configurations and system oversight.
Organizational policies and practices foster critical thinking and safety-first mindset in AI design, development, deplo
Teams document risks and potential impacts of AI technology; communicate impacts broadly.
Organizational practices enable AI testing, incident identification, and information sharing.
Policies and practices in place to collect, consider, prioritize, and integrate external feedback regarding potential in
Mechanisms established to regularly incorporate adjudicated feedback from relevant AI actors into system design and impl
Policies and procedures address AI risks associated with third-party entities, including intellectual property infringem
Contingency processes in place to handle failures or incidents in high-risk third-party data or AI systems.
Determination made whether AI system achieves intended purpose and stated objectives; whether development or deployment
Treatment of documented AI risks prioritized based on impact, likelihood, or available resources or methods.
Responses to high-priority risks developed, planned, and documented (mitigate, transfer, avoid, or accept).
Negative residual risks to downstream acquirers and end users documented.
Resources required to manage AI risks taken into account, along with viable non-AI alternatives.
Mechanisms in place and applied to sustain value of deployed AI systems.
Procedures followed to respond to and recover from previously unknown risk when identified.
Mechanisms in place and responsibilities assigned to supersede, disengage, or deactivate AI systems demonstrating incons
AI risks and benefits from third-party resources regularly monitored; risk controls applied and documented.
Pre-trained models used for development monitored as part of regular AI system monitoring and maintenance.
Post-deployment monitoring plans implemented, including mechanisms for capturing end-user input, appeal and override, de
Measurable activities for continual improvements integrated into AI system updates; regular engagement with interested p
Incidents and errors communicated to relevant AI actors including affected communities; processes for tracking, respondi
Intended purpose, beneficial uses, context-specific laws, norms, expectations, and deployment settings understood and do
Interdisciplinary AI actors with diverse competencies and documentation of their participation.
Organization's mission and relevant goals for the AI technology understood and documented.
Business value or context of business use clearly defined or re-evaluated.
Organizational risk tolerances determined and documented.
System requirements elicited from and understood by relevant AI actors; design decisions consider socio-technical implic
Specific task and implementation methods that the AI system will support are defined.
Information about AI system's knowledge limits and how output may be utilized and overseen by humans documented.
Scientific integrity and Test, Evaluation, Validation, and Verification (TEVV) considerations identified and documented.
Potential benefits of intended AI system functionality and performance examined and documented.
Potential costs (including non-monetary) resulting from expected or realized AI errors examined and documented.
Targeted application scope specified and documented based on system capability and established context.
Processes for operator and practitioner proficiency with AI system performance defined, assessed, and documented.
Processes for human oversight defined, assessed, and documented in accordance with organizational policies.
Approaches for mapping AI technology and legal risks of components in place, followed, and documented, including third-p
Internal risk controls for AI system components, including third-party technologies, identified and documented.
Likelihood and magnitude of each identified impact examined and documented based on expected use, similar contexts, publ
Practices and personnel supporting regular engagement with relevant AI actors and integrating feedback about positive, n
Approaches and metrics for measurement of enumerated AI risks selected, starting with most significant risks. Risks that
Appropriateness of AI metrics and effectiveness of existing controls regularly assessed and updated, including error rep
Internal experts not serving as front-line developers and/or independent assessors involved in regular assessments. Doma
Test sets, metrics, and tool details used during TEVV documented.
Privacy risk of the AI system examined and documented.
Fairness and bias evaluated; results documented.
Environmental impact and sustainability of AI model training and management assessed and documented.
Effectiveness of employed TEVV metrics and processes evaluated and documented.
Evaluations involving human subjects meet applicable requirements and are representative of relevant population.
AI system performance or assurance criteria measured qualitatively or quantitatively and demonstrated for deployment-sim

AI system demonstrated to be valid and reliable; limitations of generalizability documented.
AI system regularly evaluated for safety risks; demonstrated to be safe with residual risk not exceeding tolerance; can

Risks associated with transparency and accountability examined and documented.
AI model explained, validated, and documented; output interpreted within context to inform responsible use and governanc
Approaches, personnel, and documentation in place to regularly identify and track existing, unanticipated, and emergent
Risk tracking approaches considered for settings where AI risks are difficult to assess using current measurement techni
Feedback processes for end users and impacted communities to report problems and appeal outcomes established and integra
Measurement approaches for identifying AI risks connected to deployment context(s) and informed through consultation wit
Measurement results regarding AI system trustworthiness validated through input from domain experts and relevant AI acto
Measurable performance improvements or declines identified and documented based on relevant actor consultations and fiel
